element_introduction_order.mastery_context: foundational system that affects all future gameplay options and perk availability
up to 2 traits with dual positive/negative effects; 18 learnable skills define playstyle approach
core loop of world traversal and dialogue; dialogue length varies by character
action point economy determines turn efficiency; weapon switching and positioning critical
gold coins and item trading unlock equipment and quest solutions
configurable inventory, weapons, armor, and combat style; dog companions have restrictions
positive reputation unlocks quests and NPC cooperation; negative causes hostility
no hard time limit but hometown reputation decays; foundational story beat
diplomacy, combat, stealth, or unconventional approaches; yield experience and rewards
69 perks plus 10 special perks with specific prerequisites; gates advanced capabilities
tracks alignment; dictates NPC treatment and available story/gameplay opportunities
wearable computer interface for quest management and inventory
culminating story objective with no time limit
13 in-game years available for exploration before engine auto-termination

typical_tension_triggers: reputation decay in hometown during main quest delay
karma consequences from morally ambiguous choices
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: player-paced exploration with encounter-driven tension; relaxation tied to successful negotiation and reward loops; no hard time limit on main quest 1 but social pressure via reputation decay creates soft deadline

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: side quests and faction reputation gates offer optional areas and story branches; companion recruitment is optional

difficulty_curve.early_game: character creation and skill selection establish baseline capabilities; first encounters introduce turn-based action economy in low-pressure NPC dialogue contexts
difficulty_curve.mid_game: faction reputation mechanics and multi-solution quests increase complexity; companion recruitment and perk progression expand tactical options; side quest density allows difficulty scaling via selection
difficulty_curve.late_game: main quest 2 requires synthesis of all systems; reputation consequences become critical; karma alignment affects available story paths
notable_spikes: first turn-based combat encounter (action point economy unfamiliar)
early faction interactions with reputation consequences
perk prerequisite gates (level 15+ requirements lock advanced builds)
main quest 2 convergence of multiple faction alignments

notable_level_patterns.pattern_name: Settlement hub with radiant quest generation
notable_level_patterns.description: each major settlement offers NPC dialogue trees with multiple quest givers; reputation system gates new questlines and NPC cooperation; character dialogue length varies to signal importance
notable_level_patterns.example_level: player's hometown (reputation decay pressure); major faction cities (reputation rewards/locks)

notable_level_patterns.description: quests support diplomacy, combat, stealth, or unconventional approaches; encourages replay and experimentation; NPC interaction-heavy rather than space-puzzle-based
up to 14 recruitable companions with configurable equipment and combat AI; supports tactical combat variation and narrative reactivity
notable_level_patterns.example_level: active party during exploration and combat

notable_level_patterns.description: action point allocation during player turn creates decision-making tension; weapon switching mid-combat and area-targeting add complexity layers
